{{Quote|Master Fisto, in these matters, trust your insight we do.|Yoda, briefing Kit Fisto before the Battle of Mon Calamari|<ref name="Clone Wars Chapter 5">{{CW|5}}</ref>}}
'''Kit Fisto''' was a renowned [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] in the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]] he took [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]] as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. He later trained [[Nahdar Vebb/Legends|Nahdar Vebb]] as his Padawan to knighthood as well. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], he served as a [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], as well as a member of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].

Fisto participated in and survived the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] and led a team in the assault during the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends|Battle of Mon Calamari]]. Fisto was also present at Kamino, where he pushed the limits of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] with his relationship with fellow Jedi [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], whose life he saved during the battle. On a [[Mission to Grievous's castle/Legends|mission]] to recapture [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], Fisto came face to face with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], nearly defeating the Jedi killer. However, the tide of the battle changed when Grievous activated his personal [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|droid bodyguards]] and then slew Fisto's newly Knighted former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Nahdar Vebb/Legends|Nahdar Vebb]]. At the height of the Clone Wars, he assisted [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]] and subsequently dueled with [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] in order to stop the manufacturing of the [[JK-13 security droid|Jedi Killer]] droids. Finally, after years of being considered for a seat on the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]], Fisto was appointed to the august body due to his exemplary achievements during the Clone Wars.

As the war continued, Fisto was appointed to the Jedi Council. He was later dispatched with Obi-Wan Kenobi and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]] on a [[Siege of Cato Neimoidia|mission]] to capture [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], who barely escaped the encounter with the three famous Jedi. During the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], Fisto aided in the aerial battle against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] invasion forces. Upon Anakin Skywalker's revelation that [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was in fact Darth Sidious, Fisto, along with [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]] and [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], attempted to arrest the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], but failed. In that final [[Showdown on Coruscant|confrontation]] with the scheming [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], Fisto [[Death/Legends|perished]] at the hands of Darth Sidious.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|Hello, Master Qui-Gon, Obi-Wan. I have heard much about you from my Padawan. I am pleased to have the opportunity to speak with you, though I am afraid what we will be discussing will not be pleasant.|Kit Fisto meets [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] for the first time|<ref name="Followers">''[[Jedi Apprentice Special Edition: The Followers]]''</ref>}}
Kit Fisto was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolan]] born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Glee Anselm/Legends|Glee Anselm]], in the [[Sabilon]] region.<ref name="New Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> He was related to the Force-sensitive [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]] master [[Neaed Fisto]], who was his mother's brother.<ref name="Shadow Games">''[[Shadow Games]]''</ref> [[Jedi training/Legends|Trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], his distinctive head tentacles contained highly sensitive olfactory receptors that allowed him to detect [[Pheromone/Legends|pheromonal]] expressions of emotion and other changes in body chemistry. This was indeed a unique attribute not afforded to any other species of Jedi, either naturally or through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Constructing a green-bladed [[Kit Fisto's lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and completing the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]], Kit Fisto ascended to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Not one to draw attention to himself, Jedi Fisto studied the path of the [[Jedi Consular]], aspiring to gain a seat on the [[Council of Reconciliation/Legends|Council of Reconciliation]] in the future.<ref name="Power of the Jedi" />

When Master [[Tahl]] was murdered in [[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]],<ref name="Death of Hope">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope]]''</ref> Jedi Fisto took over the training of the young [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]].<ref name="Followers" /> While he did not know if he was ready for an apprentice of his own, Fisto decided that having an amphibious master would aid Eerin in her growth and recovery from her loss. Training Eerin to knighthood, Jedi Fisto was in no hurry to take another Padawan, preferring to attend to his duties at the Temple.<ref name="Power of the Jedi" /> However, another Padawan would be in Fisto's future shortly after being raised to the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. [[Nahdar Vebb/Legends|Nahdar Vebb]], a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] male entered into an apprenticeship with Fisto, learning the ways of the Force from the quiet Nautolan Jedi.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />

In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], Masters Mace Windu and Kit Fisto responded to an emergency call by Obi-Wan Kenobi to provide backup for the liberation of the Fondor system from the machinations of [[Groodo]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and [[Fondorian]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Rodd]]. The two High Council members were critical to the retaking of the [[Fondor Spaceport]]. They had been attending the dedication of a memorial to former High Council member [[Yarael Poof/Legends|Yarael Poof]] on [[Quermia/Legends|Quermia]] when they intercepted Kenobi's request for reinforcements.<ref name="Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess">''[[Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess]]''</ref>

Fisto joined Windu on many missions over their careers, including a dangerous incident on the [[Moons of Aldin]].<ref name="RH">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Republic Heroes]]''</ref> Around the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Master Fisto and his colleague [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] served as personal [[Jedi Ambassador|Jedi Advisors]] to the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="ImageAttack">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030923.html|series=Image Attack|text=Jedi Advisors}}</ref> a role that had once been filled by the late Master [[Jorus C'baoth]] when Palpatine was still a mere [[Senator/Legends|senator]].<ref name="RPGRCR">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]''</ref>

===Clone Wars===
====Battle of Geonosis====
[[File:KitGeonosis.png|thumb|230px|Kit Fisto leads a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in the Battle of Geonosis.]]
Soon after dispatching the [[Iridium Pirates/Legends|Pirates of Iridium]] in early 22 BBY with the aid of Master [[Belsed-Qan Idan]], Fisto was one of the [[Jedi assault team/Legends|two hundred Jedi]] that accompanied [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] to rescue Jedi [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], and Senator [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] from execution at the hands of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] leadership. Landing on the dusty plains of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], Master Fisto and his comrades stormed the [[Petranaki arena]] and ignited their lightsabers amidst hundreds of droids. During the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle in the arena]], Fisto crossed paths with a [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] equipped with the head of a [[C-3PO/Legends|protocol droid]]; he pinned him underneath a [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]] before grinning to himself and continuing on with the fight.<ref name="Attack of the Clones">{{Film|II}}</ref>

One of the few Jedi standing when the survivors were shepherded into the center of the arena, Fisto stood ready to die with his allies when several [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] dropped out of the sky. Piloted by the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the newly formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Fisto boarded Grand Master Yoda's ship along with fellow survivors Masters Windu and [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]. Landing on the plains outside the arena, Fisto led the new Grand Army against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]] during what would be known as the opening battle of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Attack of the Clones" />

====Battle of Mon Calamari====
{{Main|Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends}}
[[File:Kit_cartoon.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Fisto fighting in the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends|Battle of Mon Calamari]].]]
Having taken up the mantle of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the ranks of the Grand Army, Fisto was assigned to lead troops on many fronts. In one of his first Clone Wars campaigns, Fisto led a team of [[Clone SCUBA trooper/Legends|clone SCUBA troopers]] in the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends|Battle of Mon Calamari]] against the Separatist-allied [[Quarren Isolation League/Legends|Quarren Isolation League]], destroying a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[crab cannon]] in the process.<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars">''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''</ref>

Fisto later journeyed back to [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] upon discovering that the Isolation League had come back into being, under the leadership of strange jellyfish-like creatures known as [[Moappa]]. These beings grouped together to become one great beast, possessing superlative intelligence and the power of [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]]. Fisto defeated this being by scattering the individual creatures throughout the waters. Fisto later forged a new accord on the world that led to joint Quarren-[[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] representation in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].<ref name="Fierce Currents">{{CWACite|1|Fierce Currents}}</ref>

====Skirmish on Kamino====
[[File:KitFisto-swrep.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Fisto during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].]]
A few months after the Battle of Geonosis, Fisto and Aayla Secura were assigned to oversee the training of clones on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. While in [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]] the Jedi found several dead clone troopers amongst the city's polished halls. After examination by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] scientists, they concluded that the troopers were infected by a [[Kaminoan nano-virus|nano-virus]]. After visiting Master Cloner [[Sayn Ta]], the Jedi pair deduced that there was a Kaminoan traitor in the facility. Fisto and Secura traced the traitor and faced off against a group of super battle droids, which were given to the traitor by her allies in the Confederacy. In the resulting skirmish, Secura was thrown off a platform and dragged down into the water by one of the destroyed battle droids. Fisto quickly jumped after her and, being amphibious, breathed for the both of them by locking lips with the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]. Secura was then able to climb up the platform only to be grabbed by the traitor, with the nano-virus injector pointed to her arm. Revealed to be the Kaminoan [[Kuma Nai]], Secura was able to break free of Nai and pushed the injector into the traitor's own arm, killing the Kaminoan instantly.<ref name="Tides of Terror">{{Tales|14|Tides of Terror}}</ref>

====Skirmish on Devaron====
{{Main|Mission to Devaron}}
Some months later, Fisto would again be paired with Secura when the pair traveled to [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]] with fellow Jedi [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]], [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], and [[The Dark Woman|An'ya Kuro]]. Their mission was to locate and decimate a Separatist foundry launching raids on Republic supply convoys. While Tholme and Kuro lay injured and Secura was holding off [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], Fisto and Saa assaulted and captured the Confederacy's base.<ref name="jas">''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]''</ref>

====Mission on Ord Cestus====
{{Expandsect}}
{{Quote|My life does not revolve around comfort. Still… it was, as I've heard humans say, a 'close shave'.|Kit Fisto|<ref name="Cestus Deception">''[[The Cestus Deception]]''</ref>}}
{{Main|Mission to Ord Cestus}}
[[File:The_Cestus_Deception.jpg|left|thumb|274x274px|Fisto and Obi-Wan are surrounded by Ventress.]]
During the height of the Clone Wars, Fisto became the first to defeat a new type of droid that was given the name "[[JK-13 security droid|Jedi Killer]]." These droids were known for their unmatched combat abilities after demonstrating their power on an [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]], [[Sirty]], and knocking him out in twenty seconds. Chancellor Palpatine, not wanting Ord Cestus to produce an army of these droids, sends Fisto to the planet to investigate the source. Accompanying Fisto to Ord Cestus was Obi-Wan Kenobi, [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] [[Jangotat|Nate]], four clone troopers, and [[barrister]] [[Doolb Snoil]], who was a representative of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. They traveled to the planet [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]] in order to negotiate an agreement that would sell the "bio-droids" to the Republic; however, the plan soon became to recruit guerrillas to fight in a possible coup against the government. This attracted many farmers and miners whose lives were destroyed by the Five Families and wanted revenge against the government. Fisto went by the false name of "Nemonus", a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], to trick the [[Five Families of Cestus Cybernetics|Five Families]], whom the Jedi discovered to have complete control over the planet. However, the plot was discovered and both Jedi and the clone troopers were forced to retreat to the mountains west of the capital.<ref name="Cestus Deception" />

Near the end of the operation, he dueled with [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]]. She studied his moves while he took out her [[X'Ting]] minions with ease; when he came to her, he was ready. Fisto accelerated into a blur of motion complex and rapid enough to baffle even Kenobi's experienced gaze. However, he was unable to overcome Ventress and was wounded in the arm. He was saved from death by Kenobi, who arrived seconds before Ventress would have finished him. Kenobi, however, would have been beheaded if the near-unconscious Fisto had not quickly returned the favor.<ref name="Cestus Deception" />

====Duel with a cyborg====
[[File:FistoOverVassekMoon-LoG.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Fisto arrives in the [[Vassek system/Legends|Vassek system]]]]
{{Main|Mission to Grievous's castle/Legends}}
{{Quote|This is the lair of General Grievous!|Kit Fisto upon their discovery of Grievous' lair|audio=LairOfGeneralGrievous-LoG.flac|<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />}}
After [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] escaped from Master Luminara Unduli and Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]], the High Council tracked him to a [[Third moon of Vassek/Legends|moon of Vassek]] and sent Kit Fisto to capture him. There Fisto landed his [[Kit Fisto's Delta-7B Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7B]] in the fog and was reunited with his former Padawan and recently knighted, [[Nahdar Vebb/Legends|Nahdar Vebb]], who brought along clone commander [[Fil/Legends|Fil]] and a small squad of clones.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />

The group then entered a castle where Gunray was thought to be hiding, however, the lead was a ruse. The beacon led only to a hologram of Gunray laughing derisively. Gunray's image was replaced by that of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]], informing them of a trap, but not indicating whether it was for them or for someone else. They soon realized that they had entered the sanctuary of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], who happened to be approaching the citadel they were infiltrating. Grievous was caught by surprise when the Jedi and their squad of clones engaged him, and suffered the loss of his legs before he escaped and retreated to the control room of his castle, where he directed efforts to eliminate the intruders.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />

[[File:FistoDualSabers-LoG.png|thumb|left|220px|Kit Fisto faces Grievous with both his and Vebb's lightsabers]]
After escaping traps and fighting a [[Roggwart/Legends|roggwart]] named [[Gor/Legends|Gor]], which had finished off Fil, Fisto and Vebb witnessed from a hiding place the General exiting his control room. When the area was clear, Fisto entered the sequence on the security door that he remembered by watching Grievous. Fisto went to get inside of the control room. However, Vebb, feeling a strong urge to defeat the Jedi killer once and for all, disputed with Fisto from the other side of the threshold. Vebb's fate was decided when Grievous' doctor, [[EV-A4-D/Legends|EV-A4-D]], remotely sealed the door between the two Jedi and alerted Grievous. After taking care of the [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]], Fisto could only watch in despair as Vebb was shot down by Grievous' hidden [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], drawn via one of the General's free appendages. He then destroyed the console and exited the castle to a cliff precipice to wait for [[R6-H5/Legends|R6-H5]] to bring his ship.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />

However, Grievous climbed the side of the cliff to trap Fisto. The Jedi Master and the cyborg then engaged in a brief but intense [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]. Grievous took the upper hand first, destabilizing Fisto with one of his favorite approach, 'windmilling' with his two upper arms while fencing with his two lower. It wouldn't last long however: Fisto retaliated by locking Grievous' numerous blades. Fisto told Grievous that he may have once been a glorious warrior, but he was now just Dooku's pawn. Grievous responded by saying he wielded great power, then attempted to strike him down. Fisto ducked under the blade then severed one of Grievous' hands, reclaiming Vebb's lightsaber in the process. Warning that such power would only consume him, Fisto then switched to [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] and evened the odds against his ailing opponent. As he was about to pin Grievous to the cliff's wall, Grievous' [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]] suddenly appeared and surrounded him. Ignoring Grievous taunts and offer of a quick death, Fisto jumped toward his starfighter with the aid of the Force and departed, leaving a fuming Grievous behind. Once back on Coruscant, Fisto reported on the death of Jedi Vebb and his failure to arrest Grievous or Gunray to the High Council.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />

A member of the [[Old Guard]], the humble Master Fisto repeatedly declined offers of a seat on the High Council, citing that he had not the skills for such an appointment. Eventually, however, Fisto's exploits in the war answered any lingering doubts about his suitability, and he took a seat on the august body.<ref name="DB" />

====Rescue on Juma 9====
When the [[Juma 9]] station became under attack [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]], [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], Commander [[Cody/Legends|Cody]] and [[Switch (clone trooper)|Switch]] went to try and stop the conflict. While going to the comms room, Obi-Wan and Plo found a [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoan]] scientist there. They follow him, and he reveals himself to be [[Kul Teska]]. After a battle, he escapes but Obi-Wan and Plo get trapped.

Since Anakin has lost contact with Obi-Wan and Plo Koon, Fisto and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] arrived to rescue the trapped pair and succeeded.

====Battle of Khorm====
{{Quote|Patience, Captain Ozzel. No point in firing until we know exactly what we're firing at|Kit Fisto to Capt. Ozzel at the [[Battle of Khorm/Legends|Battle of Khorm]]|<ref name="Star Wars: The Clone Wars: In Service of the Republic">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: In Service of the Republic]]''</ref>}}
Fisto, along with Masters [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and [[Tauht]], [[Wolffe/Legends|Commander Wolffe]], and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel]] were sent to liberate [[Khorm/Legends|Khorm's]] people and its [[Agrocite/Legends|Agrocite]] mines from the Separatists. Here, both Fisto and Master Koon engaged Ventress once more, but defeated her and liberated this world from the Confederacy rule.<ref name="Star Wars: The Clone Wars: In Service of the Republic" />

Fisto once more joined Koon when they were dispatched to quell an uprising of inmates of [[M'Bardi Prison]]. Here they encountered the infamous [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] bounty hunter [[Durge/Legends|Durge]], whom they trapped in the catacombs beneath the prison. Fisto remained alert, thinking that Durge would escape before reinforcements arrived. Plo Koon was confident they had subdued the seemingly invincible bounty hunter, but as Fisto predicted, the bounty hunter escaped.<ref name="Means and Ends">{{CWACite|6|Means and Ends}}</ref>

====Attack on Rishi====
[[File:SWTCW_cover_art.jpg|thumb|199x199px|Fisto battling Separatist forces on Rishi|left]]
Fisto and a number of clone troopers went to Rishi to defeat a large number of Geonosians from attacking and invading an area of the planet. Fisto and clone trooper [[Cooker]] attacked the Geonosians and successfully killed them all, making Rishi free and safe once again.<ref name="Gauntlet of Death">{{StoryCite|story=The Gauntlet of Death|book=Free Comic Book Day 2009 Special|sformatted=''The Gauntlet of Death''|smanual=1}}</ref>

====Mission to Rodia====
[[File:Skirmish_on_Rodia.png|thumb|right|150px|Kit Fisto in the Rodian waters.]]
After the [[Senate hostage crisis/Legends|hostage crisis in the senate]], Fisto was sent to [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] when the Rodian wildlife began dying. Senator [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]] was afraid the Confederacy had returned and requested the Jedi investigate. Fisto searched the waters, while his [[Unidentified clone SCUBA commander|Commander]] stayed on the surface. While in the water, Master Fisto and his troops found the droid generator. It turned on the clones and fired at them, killing them. Master Fisto witnessed [[Robonino/Legends|Robonino]] leaving the scene and pursued him. The bounty hunter threw several thermal detonators at the Jedi, but Fisto redirected them in the generator, deactivating all droids on the planet. Unfortunately for the Jedi, Robonino was able to escape while Fisto was distracted. Fisto and the commander later reported to Senator Farr and told him that all droids were eliminated and the wildlife would turn back to form, but that the bounty hunter escaped. A [[Kwazel Maw/Legends|Kwazel Maw]] then spat Robonino out on the shore next to them, and he was arrested.<ref name="The Clone Wars: Hunting the Hunters Part II">[[The Clone Wars: Hunting the Hunters (Part II)|''The Clone Wars: Hunting the Hunters'' (Part II)]]</ref>

Fisto then met up with [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] and [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]] at the [[Ord Cestus medical station/Legends|Ord Cestus medical station]] where he analyzed the [[Brain worm/Legends|Brain worms]] found on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] and the ship.<ref name="Brain Invaders">{{TCW|Brain Invaders}}</ref> Fisto was also present (via hologram) at the temple when Grievous broadcast his [[Capture of Eeth Koth/Legends|capture]] of fellow council member [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]].<ref name="Grievous Intrigue">{{TCW|Grievous Intrigue}}</ref>

====Battle of Lola Sayu====
Fisto later assisted Master Koon in rescuing Master Kenobi and Jedi Skywalker's team from the [[Citadel/Legends|Citadel]] after their [[Battle of Lola Sayu/Legends|rescue]] of Master [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]]. Fisto piloted his [[Kit Fisto's Delta-7B Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|light interceptor]] to the surface, leading a squadron of troops alongside Masters [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]. Fisto fought against the blockade protecting the planet with his two allies, protecting Plo Koon in his [[Plo's Bros/Legends|gunship]] as he went toward the rendezvous to pick up Kenobi and Skywalker's team. Escorting the gunship aboard Koon's [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]], the group returned to Coruscant to debrief the High Council.<ref name="Citadel Rescue">{{TCW|Citadel Rescue}}</ref>

====Return to Mon Calamari====
[[File:Ackbar_contacts_the_Jedi.png|left|thumb|220x220px|Fisto contacts Yoda and Mace Windu before the Second Battle of Mon Calamari]]
When the peace talks between the Quarren and the Mon Calamari ended, Fisto along with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] and a company of clone SCUBA troopers were sent to Dac to aid Prince [[Lee-Char/Legends|Lee-Char's]] forces in defense of their world. After his forces landed, Fisto drove a [[OMS Devilfish sub/Legends|OMS Devilfish sub]] down to the [[Coral City/Legends|Mon Calamari City]]. Fisto and his clone forces fought against the first wave of the Quarren and Separatist forces under Commander [[Riff Tamson/Legends|Riff Tamson]]. The quarren and the [[Aqua droid/Legends|Aqua droids]] then retreated back to their ships and the coral. Fisto knew that the droids were regrouping for another attack. When the droids attacked, they sent in [[Hydroid Medusa/Legends|Hydroid Medusas]]. Fisto along with Skywalker fought against these creature, but to no avail.<ref name="Water War">{{TCW|Water War}}</ref>

When Tamson came in behind them, Lee Char, at the behest of his military adviser Captain [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], ordered a retreat to caves on the seafloor. Fisto held the hydroid medusas off by driving his mini sub into one of them, destroying it in the process. Fisto then regrouped with what remained of the Mon Calamari and Republic Forces with Skywalker, Tano, Amidala, Binks and the Prince.<ref name="Water War" />

[[File:Fistotamson.png|thumb|220x220px|Fisto fights Riff Tamson]]
When Tamson attacked Lee-Char, Fisto swam to the Prince's defense and tackled Tamson, briefly dislocating the [[Karkarodon/Legends|Karkarodon's]] lower jaw. Fisto then activated his lightsaber and went for the killing blow. However, Tamson quickly recovered and held Fisto by the arms for a few seconds before kicking him with enough force to make the Jedi lose his weapon. The odds were now even between the water-bound opponents as they [[Unarmed combat/Legends|traded unarmed blows]] against each other. Fisto ordered Tano to take Lee-Char to safety while he kept Tamson occupied. Almost immediately, Tamson had [[Aqua droid/Legends|aqua droids]] surround Fisto, effecting the Nautolan's capture.<ref name="Gungan Attack">{{TCW|Gungan Attack}}</ref>

It was not long before Skywalker, Amidala and Binks joined Fisto in imprisonment; the Jedi were each encircled by [[Mon Cala eel/Legends|Mon Cala eels]], the politicians shackled by [[Mon Cala crab/Legends|Mon Cala crabs]]. Tamson proceeded to interrogate them on Lee Char's whereabouts. When Fisto and Skywalker proved uncooperative, the [[Eel cage/Legends|eel cages]] were prodded, electrocuting the Jedi. Even with all the [[Torture/Legends|torture]], neither Jedi would break. As Tamson pierced a small hole in Amidala's helmet, threatening to slowly drown her, Fisto came up with the idea of both Jedi using the Force to expel the water. This gave Binks the chance to use his saliva to seal the hole, saving Amidala's life.<ref name="Prisoners">{{TCW|Prisoners}}</ref>

By this time, Tano and Lee Char had turned themselves in, requesting an audience with Tamson. As the prisoners waited, Fisto serenely advised that Skywalker that "less talking" would help the younger, restless Jedi's situation in the eel cage. It was only when the Quarren, led by the honorable [[Nossor Ri/Legends|Nossor Ri]], changed sides that the tied had turned. The anticipated public execution of Lee Char was halted, replaced by battle. Tano then incapacitated her droid guard and regained her lightsabers, which helped the Jedi lead the politicians away from the war zone. With Dac liberated and Tamson dead, Fisto later witnessed King Lee Char's coronation.<ref name="Prisoners" />

Fisto then helped Skywalker in arresting the Separatist sniper, Captain [[Shonn Volta]], before she was able to assassinate [[Riyo Chuchi/Legends|Riyo Chuchi]], the [[Pantoran/Legends|Pantoran]] senator.<ref name="Darth Vader & the Ghost Prison">[[Darth Vader and the Ghost Prison 3|''Darth Vader and the Ghost Prison'' 3]]</ref>

====Crimson Nova====
Eventually, the Jedi learned of a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] that had been placed on all Jedi. To end this threat, Fisto traveled with fellow Jedi Masters Windu, Tiin and [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]] to [[the Rig]]—the base of operations for the [[Crimson Nova]] chapter of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]]. While the other three Jedi were wreaking havoc on the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], Fisto made his way to the prison cells. Upon freeing the prisoners, Fisto incited an uprising of the inmates by reminding them of the wrong done to them by the bounty hunters before joining his fellow Jedi. The four soon made [[Mission to the Rig|quick work]] of the bounty hunters, forcing them to drop the price on Jedi.<ref name="Star Wars: Republic: Show of Force">''[[Star Wars: Republic: Show of Force]]''</ref>

====Battle of Cato Neimoidia====
{{Quote|Send more droids against the Jedi Knight! Their bodies are making a pile so high that he will never be able to cross it!|[[Sentepeth Findos]], speaking of Kit Fisto|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
With a large Republic counterattack against the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], General Grievous had ordered all members of the Council to retreat to a rendezvous point in the Outer Rim Territories. However, Republic Intelligence obtained information that Nute Gunray fled back to his stronghold on [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]].<ref name="LOE">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>

Fisto, accompanied by Generals Kenobi and Skywalker, [[Siege of Cato Neimoidia|led an assault]] on the Neimoidian homeworld of Cato Neimoidia. After a large aerial bombardment, the main clone force, led by General Fisto, landed and began to hammer the city's energy shields. After heavy fighting Fisto and his clones finally broke through to the citadel where Nute Gunray was hiding. However, Gunray escaped before any of the Jedi could stop him.<ref name="LOE" />

====Battle of Coruscant====
[[File:Kit_Fisto_starfighter.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Fisto's [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]].]]
Recalled to Coruscant by Masters Windu and Yoda when Coruscant was attacked by General Grievous, Fisto joined Windu in the ensuing [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|battle on the ground]]. When Grievous tried to take the Supreme Chancellor by boarding the [[Hovertrain/Legends|mag-lev train]] he was being evacuated in, Fisto and Windu pitted themselves against Grievous's strike force, saving the Chancellor and the countless civilians on board.<ref name="LOE" />

In one attempt, Grievous was so bold that he jumped onto the train and tried to pry open the roof of the car in which the Chancellor was in. Fisto was the first to realize this danger and shot through the hole Grievous had made, followed by Windu. While Windu fought Grievous, Fisto took down two MagnaGuards with ease, repulsing Grievous' attempt to take the Chancellor. However, their efforts proved to be in vain. When they heard of [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti's]] failure to protect Palpatine, they pursued Grievous' [[Tri-wing shuttle|shuttle]] in a final, unsuccessful attempt.<ref name="LOE" />

===Death===
{{Quote|Sleep, all life is a dream.|Kit Fisto|<ref name="Cestus Deception" />}}
[[File:KitFistoDeath-ROTS.png|thumb|right|200px|Fisto dies at the hands of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]].]]
In a Jedi Council meeting, the Masters were certain that Palpatine was being manipulated by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Darth Sidious.<ref name="ROTSn">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> Setting a mass plan to down the Separatists permanently, Obi Wan was sent on a risky mission to Utapau to eliminate General Grievous, with Yoda being sent to Kashyyyk to aid with the defense of the planet.<ref name="ROTS" /> It was determined that Kolar and Windu would arrest the Chancellor soon after, if he continued an aggressive stance even after Grievous' capture.<ref name="ROTSn" />

On the eve of Kolar's and Windu's confrontation with Palpatine,<ref name="ROTSn" /> Anakin Skywalker revealed the Chancellor to be the Sith Lord that the Jedi had been seeking for some time.<ref name="ROTS" /> Immediately, Windu summoned Fisto and Tiin to accompany him and Kolar to [[Showdown on Coruscant|confront]] Palpatine. Fisto briefly chatted with Kolar and Tiin before their gunship departed to the [[Chancellor's Suite/Legends|Chancellor's Suite]]. Nervous, he claimed it would have been much calmer if Yoda or Obi-Wan Kenobi had been with them, fondly recounting his mission with Obi-Wan on Ord Cestus as Kolar expressed wonder over Skywalker remaining in the temple. Master Tiin scolded him for this; he maintained that they should be capable of subduing Sidious, and that they were sufficient for the task while Windu tended to Kolar's questions.<ref name="ROTSn" />

Walking into the office, the four Jedi were astonished when the friendly visage of the Chancellor melted and a crimson lightsaber dropped into his hand.<ref name="ROTS" /> Fisto's earlier fears and uneasiness were justified when Sidious,<ref name="ROTSn" /> leaping over his desk, made short work of Kolar and Tiin, slaying them with incredible speed before they could react. Fisto joined Windu in the duel and despite surviving much longer than Kolar and Tiin,<ref name="ROTS" /> his Shii-Cho mastery was no match for Sidious' superior fighting skill. Anakin Skywalker briefly witnessed the battle between Sidious, Windu and Fisto from his speeder, as the two Jedi pressed Sidious with a flurry of attacks.<ref name="ROTSn" /> Fisto eventually met [[Death/Legends|his end]] when Sidious, the very man he had "protected" a few days before, cleaved into his stomach with his lightsaber.<ref name="ROTS" /> The fight concluded with Sidious torturing and throwing Windu out the window with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] due to Skywalker's betrayal.

===Legacy===
Not long after, Fisto's [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] uncle, [[Neaed Fisto|Neaed]], was hunted down and assassinated by the [[Anomid/Legends|Anomid]] [[Imperial Assassin]], [[Edge (Anomid)|Edge]], as part of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]].<ref name="Shadow Games" />

During his exile on [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]] the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] Jedi Knight, [[Kazdan Paratus]], erected junk puppets of the Jedi High Council members at the end of the Clone Wars. The puppet based on Kit Fisto, as with all the other puppets, was destroyed in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] by Sith Lord [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] secret apprentice, [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], when he dueled Kazdan Paratus.<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed novelization">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>

Around a year or two later, Fisto's name was brought up in conversation between [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] and [[Eaden Vrill]], a former mentor of his uncle's, who recounted his famous exploits during the Clone Wars.<ref name="Shadow Games" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Those who have power should restrain themselves from using it.|Kit Fisto|audio=ThoseWhoHavePower-LoG.flac|<ref name="Lair of Grievous" />}}
[[File:Kit_Smile.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Kit Fisto's signature smile]]
A green-skinned Nautolan of Glee Anselm, Kit Fisto wore the [[Jedi apparel/Legends|traditional robes]], as well as leather bands around his several head tails. With a laid-back and accepting outlook on life,<ref name="New Essential Guide to Characters" /> Fisto took to his duties with enthusiasm, often sporting a wide smile when events went his way.<ref name="Attack of the Clones" /> With a well-developed sense of humility to go with that sense of humor, Fisto mostly kept a low profile, initially uninterested in the idea of appointment to the High Council. Instead, he believed he would be better suited for the [[Council of Reconciliation/Legends|Council of Reconciliation]],<ref name="Power of the Jedi" /> in line with his view that it was the role of the Jedi to use the power they had been given to ensure the social betterment of those less fortunate.<ref name="New Essential Guide to Characters" />

[[File:AaylaKit.jpg|thumb|Fisto saves [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura's]] life.]]
Despite his radical combat style and easygoing nature, Fisto was surprisingly conservative on the matter of galactic politics.<ref name="DB" /> He was a determined and resourceful Jedi and was dedicated to maintaining peace throughout the galaxy and defending the honor of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. Even though he considered himself well suited to the job of training Bant Eerin, He still harbored doubts about his competence as a teacher and a role model.<ref name="Power of the Jedi" />

Fisto shared a close relationship with the Jedi Knight [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], one which, had they both not dedicated their life to the Jedi, likely would have gone even further.<ref name="DB" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|I should have gone closer to the edge. Released myself into the Force, become more unpredictable. More random.''"<br />"''That would have been dangerous. Not to your body, perhaps, but to your spirit.''"<br />"''It is the way of Form I.|Kit Fisto and Obi-Wan Kenobi discuss Shii-Cho|<ref name="Cestus Deception" />}}
[[File:KitFisto-HtH2.png|left|thumb|180px|Kit Fisto, a master of [[Form I/Legends|Form I]].]]
A master practitioner of [[Form I/Legends|Shii-Cho]],<ref name="Cestus Deception" /> Kit Fisto was considered to be one of the best swordsmen in the Jedi Order.<ref name="ROTSn" /> His mastery of the form was advanced, and he applied it in many battles and duels, always with his trademark smile. He went head to head with many of the most dangerous adversaries of the Clone Wars and survived. A notable opponent was General Grievous, whom Fisto almost defeated,<ref name="Lair of Grievous" /> as Form I's specialization against multiple foes,<ref name="KOTOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> or in this case, multiple weapons, was well-suited to holding off the four lightsabers Grievous' bizarre mechanical anatomy allowed him to wield. Fisto's quick thinking and speedy reflexes enabled him to recognize vulnerable body zones on his opponents and react immediately, as shown when he cut off Grievous' legs and later one of his hands during their duels.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" /> Even so, Grievous had overwhelmed both Fisto and Nadhar Vebb in the first engagement, and Fisto managed to fight evenly with Grievous only after the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] had recently been patched up.<ref name="Lair of Grievous" /><ref name="Grievous Attacks">''[[The Clone Wars: Grievous Attacks!]]''</ref>

However, Fisto was not without his weaknesses. Form I was ideal when heavily outnumbered and effective against many opponents, but a single opponent was able to find flaw in it,<ref name="KOTOR2" /> and this proved to be Kit Fisto's downfall. When he had fought Asajj Ventress on Ord Cestus, she had not only studied his fighting style beforehand;<ref name="Cestus Deception" /> Count Dooku had taught her [[Form II/Legends|Form II]],<ref name="Cloak of Darkness">{{TCW|Cloak of Darkness}}</ref> which was superior to Shii-Cho.<ref name="Fightsaber">{{InsiderCite|62|Fightsaber: Jedi Lightsaber Combat}}</ref> Thus, she had penetrated Fisto's guard and nearly killed him.<ref name="Cestus Deception" /> Fisto's final battle against Darth Sidious ended with his death, as—despite Fisto's own power and skill—he simply wasn't able to compete with Sidious. However, he managed to last longer than his fellow combatants, Agen Kolar and Saesee Tiin, being able to block several of Sidious' vicious blows before falling.<ref name="ROTS" />

Fisto also proved to be competent in the use of [[Form IV/Legends|Form IV]] lightsaber combat,<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> which would fit in well with his acrobatics.<ref name="Republic 66">[[Republic 66|''Republic'' 66]]</ref> His versatility and weapon skill was such that he could ably apply it towards the usage of [[Jar'Kai/Legends|dual-blades]], or even [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhips]].<ref name="Lair of Grievous" /><ref name="Cestus Deception" /> He would occasionally wield a reverse [[Form V/Legends|Shien]] grip whenever possible.<ref name="Means and Ends" /><ref name="Gauntlet of Death" /> Aside from his bladework, Fisto was reasonably adept in unarmed combat, as demonstrated by his underwater bout with Riff Tamson;<ref name="Gungan Attack" /> it was a method that he would prefer when defeating foes in a non-lethal way, though Plo Koon would criticize it as being "too slow."<ref name="Means and Ends" />

[[File:Ep2kitfistosaber.jpg|right|thumb|266x266px|[[Kit Fisto's lightsaber/Legends|Kit Fisto's lightsaber]]]]
Fisto's lightsaber contained two [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystals]] employing a bifurcating cyclical-ignition pulse that allowed the blade to operate underwater. This proved helpful on [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He wielded a green blade, the traditional color of Consulars.

Having been trained as a [[Jedi Consular]],<ref name="Power of the Jedi" /> Master Fisto was adept in the use of the Force. For instance, he was capable of creating and manipulating water currents through use of the Force for both offensive and defensive purposes. Fisto developed his own Force technique from this ability, aptly named the [[Force Orb/Legends|Force water orb]], which he used during the battle of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. He was the only known practitioner of this power, having created it himself. He also possessed an acute olfactory sense, allowing him to read the emotions of other sentients.<ref name="New Essential Guide to Characters" />

Fisto was also skilled in the use of [[Force speed/Legends|Force speed]] and on Ord Cestus, his Force enhanced speed baffled even Obi-Wan Kenobi's experienced gaze.<ref name="Cestus Deception" /> Fisto was also an extremely skilled swimmer and honed his Jedi abilities to take advantage of his amphibious origins, making him a lethal foe underwater as well as on land. Also, he was skilled in hand-to-hand combat, holding his own against [[Riff Tamson/Legends|Riff Tamson]] in an underwater brawl although he was unable to defeat him.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Kit_Fisto_artwork_Nielsen.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Kit Fisto wielding two lightsabers.]]
Kit Fisto first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] during the Battle of Geonosis. Originally, his character was designed as a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], but since the movie did not call for a Sith of a species other than [[Human/Legends|Human]], this was changed.<ref name="DB" /> [[Zachariah Jensen|Zac Jensen]] and ILMer [[Daniel Zizmor]] played Fisto in ''Attack of the Clones'', but did not replay Fisto in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']]. The role went to stuntman [[Ben Cooke]].

In the [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization of ''Revenge of the Sith'']], Fisto was beheaded by Palpatine, not cleaved into the waist. His death was not described firsthand—instead, Anakin found Fisto's head on the Chancellor's table with the Nautolan's typical smile on it. In the novel ''Labyrinth of Evil'', James Luceno incorrectly states that Fisto carries a blue lightsaber.

Nick Gillard, the lightsaber stuntman who played [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]] in ''Revenge of the Sith'', personally liked Fisto's lightsaber hilt so much that he made it his own for his character.

In ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series Kit Fisto has been voiced by [[Phil LaMarr]]. His voice was inspired by the voice of "the 7-UP guy" from its television commercials.<ref>TheForce.net Force Cast, Interview with [[Dave Filoni]].</ref> LaMarr voices the character with a Jamaican accent, even though in [[Chapter 21 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Clone Wars Chapter 21]], the first source in which Fisto speaks, the character has a Scottish accent.

Kit Fisto is shown with eyelids in the episode of ''The Clone Wars'' [[Lair of Grievous]], despite statements in published works stating Nautolans have no eyelids.

==Non-canon duel==
In [[Star Wars: Head-to-Head]], Fisto was matched up in a hypothetical duel with [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] and it was determined that Kit would prove victorious.
